head_commit.message: ci: upgrade Node.js to 22 and trigger workflow run
ci: upgrade Node.js to 22 and trigger workflow run

data: update player club data from wc26 CSV

Refresh player-clubs.json with current club assignments for all 1246
players. 1005 clubs updated via name-matched CSV import, plus 9 manual
patches for name-variant mismatches (Robertson, Itakura, Grimaldo, etc.).

Notable fixes:
- Madueke: Chelsea → Arsenal
- Son Heung-min: Tottenham → LAFC
- Modric: Real Madrid → AC Milan
- Alejandro Grimaldo: wrong Bayern Munich → Bayer Leverkusen
- Nico González: Porto → Atlético Madrid
- Andy Robertson: (empty) → Liverpool
- 100s of previously empty club fields now populated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01YCtQd4mPfw6tZd6GiwVJ5K

fix(knockout): use ESPN advanced stat as sole clinch source of truth

Switch from note.description regex matching to the ESPN 'advanced' stat
(value === 1) as the authoritative signal that a team has mathematically
clinched qualification. note.description is a display label for current
rank position and is NOT proof of clinching.

- fetchGroupStandings: replace clinched flag with advancedOfficial from
  getStat(stats, ['advanced','ADV','adv']) === 1
- updateKnockoutFromGroups: fail closed — only fill slots where
  advancedOfficial is true, never fill from note text
- Add clearAllKOSlots / clearKOSlot / getDefaultSlotLabel helpers so
  bracket resets cleanly before each refresh
- fillKOSlot: remove delete bt.dataset.slot so slots remain findable
  on subsequent refreshes
- Remove clinched tiebreaker from sort (advancedOfficial is not a
  valid ranking criterion)

Expected result: Official advanced count = 3 (USA, Mexico, Germany).
If ESPN advanced stat is absent for a team, that slot stays TBD.

Enable dynamic bracket; fix tied-group sort with clinched tiebreaker

Group K: Congo DR and Portugal tied on Pts/GD/GF (1/0/1).
Congo DR has 'Advance to Round of 32'; Portugal has 'Best 8 advance'.
Without a tiebreaker, Portugal sorted above Congo DR and blocked slot 2K.

Fix: add (b.clinched - a.clinched) as final sort criterion so clinched
teams always rank above non-clinched teams when stats are equal.

Result: all 24 slots should now fill correctly from ESPN live data.
Same fix applied to group-debug.html sort for consistency.

- vercel.json: adds build entry and /group-debug route

Add debug panel to knockout tab for live troubleshooting

Shows a table of all teams from GROUPS_DATA after ESPN loads:
pts, gd, gf, p, clinched flag, ESPN note, expected slot key,
and whether that slot is filled or still empty in the DOM.
Renders after every refreshKnockoutBracket() call.

Fills slots independently per team (no 'both must clinch' gate).
Uses Number() coercion and spread-copy sort to avoid mutation.

Hardcode knockout bracket to show only Mexico, USA, Germany

Temporary fix while dynamic ESPN-based logic is debugged.
updateKnockoutFromGroups now fills only the three confirmed
qualifiers directly by slot key, bypassing all clinched/sort logic.

Also includes: multi-name getStat aliases, Number() sort coercion,
raw entry.stats debug log, robust note parsing from prior commits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01YCtQd4mPfw6tZd6GiwVJ5K
debug: show all 48 standing notes, not just first 20

Implement engineering brief: fix knockout bracket population

- Add refreshKnockoutBracket() — always renderKnockout() then
  updateKnockoutFromGroups() in one atomic call, so the DOM is
  never filled before it exists or wiped after it is filled
- Fill Winner/Runner-up slots independently — no longer skips a
  whole group just because only one team has clinched
- Robust ESPN note parsing: checks description, shortDescription,
  displayValue, and string note fields; stores clinched/eliminated/
  bestEightAdvance/note on each team object
- Add projected styling (opacity + "Proj." label) when group not
  yet complete but team has clinched
- Add console.log in fillKOSlot (slotsFound, key, team) and
  console.table of full GROUPS_DATA after standings load for
  easy browser DevTools debugging
- Remove stray updateKnockoutQualifiers() from renderGroups() and
  replace all bare renderKnockout() calls with refreshKnockoutBracket()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01YCtQd4mPfw6tZd6GiwVJ5K

Fix clinched regex — exclude 'Best 8 advance' 3rd-place teams

ESPN uses two distinct notes:
  'Advance to Round of 32' = confirmed top-2 qualifier
  'Best 8 advance'         = 3rd-place wildcard (not confirmed)

The previous regex matched both because 'advanc' appears in 'advance'.
Now only 'Advance to Round of 32' sets clinched=true.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01YCtQd4mPfw6tZd6GiwVJ5K

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01YCtQd4mPfw6tZd6GiwVJ5K

Fix knockout bracket showing all teams instead of only qualified ones

Root cause: ESPN standings API returns gamesPlayed=3 for ALL teams
(it represents total scheduled group-stage games, not games played).
This made every group appear 'done' and filled all 24 slots with
whoever currently leads each group — regardless of whether any
match had actually been played.

Fix 1 (fetchGroupStandings): compute p = w + d + l instead of
reading gamesPlayed. Wins + draws + losses is a mathematical identity
for actual games played and cannot be inflated by scheduled fixtures.

Fix 2 (computeStandingsFromScoreboard): call renderKnockout() after
updating GROUPS_DATA, which fully rebuilds the bracket from scratch.
This guarantees any slots incorrectly filled by a prior bad run are
reset before correct slots are filled.

Fix 3 (knockout tab click): also calls renderKnockout() for the same
reason — full rebuild beats patching over potentially dirty state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01YCtQd4mPfw6tZd6GiwVJ5K

Compute group standings from scoreboard data, not just standings API

The ESPN standings API was unreliable for updating p/w/d/l values,
leaving completed groups undetected and knockout slots empty.

computeStandingsFromScoreboard() fetches 25 days of scoreboard data
(the same endpoint that already powers the match cards) and derives
standings directly from completed match results. In a 4-team group,
when all teams reach p=3 via this computation, updateKnockoutQualifiers
fills the bracket slots automatically.

This runs in parallel with fetchGroupStandings so both sources feed
the bracket.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01YCtQd4mPfw6tZd6GiwVJ5K

opening the team page. Only the player row itself should be tappable

renderers and the hover cursor style on .lb-team.

Extend match history window to cover full group stage

fetchMatchSpotlight was only looking back 5 days, so matches played
in the first week of the group stage (Jun 11-16) were invisible.
Extended to -20/+14 days so all completed group stage matches show
in the schedule list below the spotlight card.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01YCtQd4mPfw6tZd6GiwVJ5K

Separate team page from My Team tab — non-favourite teams open in modal

Clicking any team that isn't the user's favourite now opens a dedicated
team info modal (reusing existing #mcModal infrastructure) instead of
hijacking the My Team tab. The My Team tab is now exclusively for the
user's chosen favourite team.

Key changes:
- openTeamPage() routes to My Team tab only for the user's favourite;
  all other teams go to new openTeamModal()
- openTeamModal() renders full team info (spotlight, standings, stats,
  squad, news) inside #mcModal with no "Change" button
- renderCompactStandings() now takes a separate viewedTeam (row
  highlight) and favTeam (You badge) param so the two concepts are
  cleanly separated — in My Team tab both are the favourite; in the
  modal the "You" badge still points to the user's actual favourite
  even when viewing a different team in the same group
- fetchMatchSpotlight, fetchTeamMatchStats, fetchFavNews all accept
  optional container ID params so they work in both tab and modal
